message:
Implement CLI logout, whoami commands with device code flow and token refresh

Adds comprehensive CLI authentication infrastructure with three major features:

## New Commands (auth.ts)

### jfp logout
- Clears locally stored credentials from ~/.config/jfp/credentials.json
- Optional --revoke flag to also invalidate the token on the server
- Confirms logout success or reports if user was not logged in
- Supports --json output for scripting

### jfp whoami
- Displays current authentication status and user information
- Shows email, subscription tier (free/premium), and token expiry
- Detects authentication via JFP_TOKEN env var vs stored credentials
- Graceful handling when not logged in
- Supports --json output for programmatic access

## Device Code Flow (login.ts)

Implements RFC 8628 OAuth 2.0 Device Authorization Grant for remote/SSH login:

- User runs `jfp login --remote` on headless server
- CLI requests device code from /api/cli/device-code endpoint
- Displays user-friendly code (e.g., "XYZW-1234") and verification URL
- Polls /api/cli/device-token every 2 seconds for up to 2 minutes
- Handles standard OAuth errors: authorization_pending, slow_down, expired_token
- Saves credentials on successful authentication
- Full JSON output support for automation

## Automatic Token Refresh (credentials.ts)

Adds transparent token refresh when access tokens expire:

- New refreshAccessToken() function calls /api/cli/refresh endpoint
- Automatically updates stored credentials with new tokens
- getAccessToken() attempts refresh before returning null for expired tokens
- Debug logging available via JFP_DEBUG=1 environment variable
- Graceful fallback: if refresh fails, user is prompted to re-login

## Security Considerations

- Credentials stored with 0600 permissions (user read/write only)
- Config directory created with 0700 permissions
- Atomic writes via temp file to prevent corruption
- Refresh tokens stored securely alongside access tokens
- No sensitive data logged (tokens are never printed)

Files changed:
- packages/cli/src/commands/auth.ts (new, 167 lines)
- packages/cli/src/commands/login.ts (+259 lines for device code flow)
- packages/cli/src/lib/credentials.ts (+58 lines for token refresh)
- packages/cli/src/index.ts (+11 lines to register new commands)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
